evidence.title: White House release: Rubio says Venezuela operation was not invasion or extended military operation
evidence.source_description: Official White House release opened with web.open on 2026-06-30. Text below preserves the material page heading, date, and bullet excerpts relevant to whether U.S. forces remained on the ground, whether the operation was an invasion or extended military operation, and whether it was framed as a war against Venezuela.
evidence.relevance: Official White House statement limiting the January 2026 Venezuela operation: no U.S. forces currently on the ground, forces were on the ground about two hours to capture Maduro, not a war against Venezuela, not an invasion, and not an extended military operation. Relevant to the proposition's carveouts for capture without territorial occupation and raids without territorial objectives.

evidence.title: White House fact sheet: Safeguards Venezuelan Oil Revenue
evidence.source_description: Official White House fact sheet; HTML retrieved locally and text extracted with Python HTMLParser. Page metadata showed published Jan. 10, 2026 and modified Jan. 10, 2026.
evidence.relevance: Official White House statement after the operation discussing U.S. custody of Venezuelan oil revenues, U.S. objectives, and characterizing the prior week operation as an extraordinary military operation in support of law enforcement.

evidence.title: Department of War transcript: Hegseth describes Caracas action as grabbing an indicted individual
evidence.source_description: Official Department of War transcript opened through web.open on 2026-06-30 from Defense.gov search result redirecting to War.gov. Text preserves page title/context and material lines describing the Caracas action.
evidence.relevance: Official Department of War transcript characterizing the January 2026 Caracas action as nearly 200 Americans going downtown and grabbing an indicted individual wanted by American justice in support of law enforcement. Relevant to whether the objective was a limited capture/law-enforcement raid rather than territorial occupation or control.

evidence.title: White House OMB Statement of Administration Policy on S.J. Res. 98
evidence.source_description: Official White House/OMB PDF opened through web renderer and downloaded locally; local PDF size 159,669 bytes, SHA-256 ee2cf252a56c4c345e913bc8dde77640b6b930e095b7488d1906d52ec4b70678. Text extracted via web.open because pdftotext/pip were unavailable.
evidence.relevance: Official White House statement addressing Jan. 3, 2026 U.S. Armed Forces hostilities within Venezuela; relevant to whether the action was targeted/limited and law-enforcement-related, an important adverse/limiting source.

evidence.title: Department of War article: Trump Announces U.S. Military's Capture of Maduro
evidence.source_description: Official U.S. Department of War/War.gov news article rendered through web.open after direct curl returned HTTP 403; source page identifies itself as official .gov and Department of War news.
evidence.relevance: Primary official U.S. defense source addressing Operation Absolute Resolve, U.S. military extraction in Caracas, ground-based extraction force, and stated U.S. oversight after Maduro's removal.
